title: Curriculum Design: Special Programs
description: This policy governs how the school district designs and delivers special academic programs, including support for students with dyslexia and other learning differences. It applies to students who may need specialized instruction beyond the standard curriculum, as well as the educators and administrators responsible for providing those services. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines the district's commitments and legal obligations for identifying and supporting students with unique learning needs, helping ensure their children receive appropriate educational assistance.

timeline.summary: This policy update revises the dyslexia compliance language to simplify the reference from lengthy state rules/standards/commissioner guidance to simply 'state rules regarding students with dyslexia and related disorders,' and adds a specific reference to The Dyslexia Handbook as an included resource. The update streamlines the compliance language while adding a concrete reference document.
changes_added: state rules regarding students with dyslexia and related disorders.
This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_removed: rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test

timeline.notes: Date issued is 2023-10-30, Version/Update 1. The redline appears in red text in the scanned document. The original sentence read: 'The District shall comply with all state rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test students for dyslexia and related disorders.' The revised sentence reads: 'The District shall comply with all state rules regarding students with dyslexia and related disorders. This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.' The second paragraph about training opportunities remains unchanged.

timeline.summary: This policy update simplifies the compliance language regarding dyslexia and related disorders. The old text referenced complying with 'rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test' students with dyslexia. The new language replaces this with a more streamlined reference to 'state' rules 'regarding' students with dyslexia and related disorders, and adds an explicit reference to the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook as the governing document.
changes_added: state (replacing the longer phrase about State Board of Education rules and commissioner guidance)
This includes the current version of The Dyslexia Handbook.
changes_removed: rules and standards adopted by the State Board of Education and guidance published by the commissioner of education to implement the program to test
